name: Resident Care & Documentation (NZS 8134 aligned)
description: Reference policy aligned with the NZ Health & Disability Services Standards (NZS 8134) and HQSC guidance.

data.text: Each resident has an individualised care plan reviewed at admission, on change of condition, and at least every 6 months.
Informed consent is documented for residency, treatment, and significant interventions; advance directives and EPOA are recorded.
Incidents (falls, medication errors, restraint, behaviour) are documented at the time, reviewed by the RN/manager, and notified to NOK and HQSC where required.
Medication administration is recorded on the MAR; controlled drugs require a witness and are reconciled per shift.
Residents living with dementia have behaviour-monitoring entries documenting triggers, interventions, and outcomes.
Resident records are retained for at least 10 years after the date of last care.

clauses.title: Individualised care plan
clauses.body: Each resident must have an individualised care plan reviewed at admission, on change of condition, and at least every 6 months.

clauses.title: Consent and advance directives recorded
clauses.body: Informed consent for residency, treatment, and significant interventions must be documented; advance directives and EPOA recorded.

clauses.title: Incidents documented and notified
clauses.body: Incidents must be documented at the time, reviewed by the RN/manager, and notified to NOK and HQSC where required.

clauses.title: MAR and controlled-drug witness
clauses.body: Medication administration must be recorded on the MAR; controlled drugs require a witness and are reconciled per shift.

clauses.title: Behaviour monitoring
clauses.body: Residents living with dementia must have behaviour-monitoring entries documenting triggers, interventions, and outcomes.

clauses.title: 10-year retention
clauses.body: Resident records must be retained at least 10 years from the date of last care.
